mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures household spending momentum, which directly feeds into corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth months are not positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate increase greater than 0.2 percentage points over a two-month window signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer income expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increase in payroll growth (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) tightens labor markets faster than productivity gains, squeezing corporate margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline do not negatively predict 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC > 0) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, industrial production (INDPRO) contractions signal an impending demand slowdown that reduces earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months with INDPRO contractions, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) growth exceeding 2.5% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to tighten financial conditions, raising equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL acceleration above 2.5% are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal durable goods demand and construction sector strength that lifts cyclical earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST changes do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling industrial production (INDPRO) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) confirms a recessionary shock that destroys earnings visibility and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns (positive mean forward return) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 600 thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment that drags down construction employment and cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 600 over any 2-year rolling window.
